question_text: Which of the following correctly describes the energy transformation that occurs when the human body uses chemical energy from food for muscle contraction?

options.label: A
options.text: Chemical energy in food is directly converted to kinetic energy with no energy loss

options.label: B
options.text: Chemical energy from food is converted to kinetic energy and heat energy through respiration in muscle cells

options.label: C
options.text: Chemical energy is stored as gravitational potential energy before being used for movement

options.label: D
options.text: Kinetic energy from food is converted to chemical energy in muscle tissue

correct_answer: Chemical energy from food is converted to kinetic energy and heat energy through respiration in muscle cells
explanation: When you eat food, your body breaks down the chemical energy stored in food molecules (like glucose) through respiration. This process releases energy that is used in two main ways: to create movement (kinetic energy) through muscle contraction, and to produce heat that keeps your body warm. Some energy is always lost as heat, so option A is incorrect because it claims no energy loss occurs.
